name: Local Food Business
description: A working APIs.json bundle for a small local food producer or farm: only the services a market-and-wholesale food operation actually needs, each carrying only the operations that matter, described inline as OpenAPI, with the signup, login, documentation, and pricing properties an owner needs to get set up, plus Arazzo workflows for the most common single-provider and cross-provider tasks. This is the machine-readable companion to the API Evangelist bundle 'Running a Local Food Business on APIs.'

apis.name: Square
apis.description: In-person and market-stall payments, catalog, orders, customers, and invoicing. The register you carry to the farmers market and the invoice you send a wholesale account.

properties.name: Square operations a food producer needs

info.title: Square (food-producer subset)
info.description: Only the Square operations a market-and-wholesale food business needs: take a sale, capture a customer, invoice a wholesale account.

post.summary: Take a payment for an order
Capture a customer at the stall

workflows.name: Square — Ring up a market sale
workflows.description: The single-provider core of a market day: build an itemized order, then take the payment against it.

info.title: Ring up a market sale
info.description: Create an order, then take a payment against it. Single provider (Square).

workflows.summary: Itemize a sale and take the payment

apis.name: Stripe
apis.description: Online payments for a food producer without a full storefront: a hosted payment link for a CSA share or a pre-order, and Checkout for a simple online cart.

properties.name: Stripe operations for selling without a storefront

info.title: Stripe (food-producer subset)
info.description: Sell a product with a shareable link: create a product and price, then a payment link.

post.summary: Create a product (a CSA share, a jar, a case)
Set the price for a product

workflows.name: Stripe — Publish a pre-order link
workflows.description: Stand up something sellable online in three calls: product, price, shareable payment link you can drop in a newsletter or on a QR code.

info.title: Publish a pre-order link
info.description: Create a product, a price, and a payment link. Single provider (Stripe).

workflows.summary: Product to price to shareable link

apis.name: QuickBooks Online
apis.description: The books: record income from a market day, invoice wholesale accounts, and keep customers in sync so tax time is not a shoebox of receipts. (Wave is a free alternative for the same job.)

properties.name: QuickBooks operations for booking food-business income

info.title: QuickBooks Online (food-producer subset)
info.description: Record income from a market day and invoice a wholesale account.

post.summary: Record a cash/market sale as income

apis.name: Shopify
apis.description: The online store when the operation outgrows a payment link: list products, take orders, and hand them off to fulfillment. Represented here with the Admin REST operations a small producer touches.

properties.name: Shopify operations for listing and fulfilling

info.title: Shopify Admin (food-producer subset)
info.description: List a product and read new orders for fulfillment.

post.summary: List a new product (e.g. a finished batch)

apis.name: Mailchimp
apis.description: The newsletter that turns a market conversation into a repeat customer: add a subscriber and send a campaign. The storytelling channel a food brand lives or dies by.

properties.name: Mailchimp operations for a producer newsletter

info.title: Mailchimp Marketing (food-producer subset)
info.description: Add a subscriber and send a campaign.

basic.description: Any username + API key as password

post.summary: Add a subscriber to your audience

workflows.name: Mailchimp — Send this week's newsletter
workflows.description: Create the campaign against your audience and send it. Single provider (Mailchimp).

info.title: Send this week's newsletter
info.description: Create a campaign and send it. Single provider (Mailchimp).

apis.name: Uber Direct
apis.description: Local, same-day courier delivery for a market or online order without owning a van: quote a delivery, then book it. The bridge from 'they came to my stall' to 'I brought it to them.'

properties.name: Uber Direct operations for local delivery

info.title: Uber Direct (food-producer subset)
info.description: Quote a delivery, then create it.

post.summary: Quote a delivery between two addresses

workflows.name: Uber Direct — Quote and book a local delivery
workflows.description: Quote a courier run between two addresses, then book it against the quote. Single provider (Uber Direct).

info.title: Quote and book a local delivery
info.description: Quote a delivery, then create it. Single provider (Uber Direct).

apis.name: USDA Local Food Directories
apis.description: Public USDA data for finding — and getting listed among — the farmers markets, food hubs, CSAs, and on-farm markets a small producer sells through. The channel map for the business.

properties.name: USDA operations for finding local sales channels

info.title: USDA Local Food Directories (subset)
info.description: Find farmers markets and CSAs near a ZIP.

get.summary: Find farmers markets near a ZIP
Find CSA operations near a ZIP

apis.name: farmOS
apis.description: Open-source, self-hostable farm records — assets, logs, and plans. The traceability substrate: log a harvest or a critical control point and own the data in your own instance, forever.

properties.name: Host or get a farmOS instance
farmOS operations for harvest logging & traceability

info.title: farmOS (food-producer subset)
info.description: Log a harvest and read plant assets — the traceability record behind a batch.

post.summary: Log a harvest (the origin record for a batch)

common.name: Market day to the books (Square → QuickBooks)
common.description: Cross-provider: take a payment at the market on Square, then record the income as a sales receipt in QuickBooks so the day is already booked before you drive home.

info.title: Market day to the books
info.description: Square payment recorded as QuickBooks income. Cross-provider.

workflows.summary: Take a payment, then book it as income

steps.description: QuickBooks: record it as a sales receipt (cents to dollars is the one honest transform here).

common.name: Online order to local courier (Shopify → Uber Direct)
common.description: Cross-provider: pull a paid online order from Shopify, then quote and book an Uber Direct courier to the customer's address. The 'I brought it to them' path.

info.title: Online order to local courier
info.description: Shopify order fulfilled by an Uber Direct delivery. Cross-provider.

workflows.summary: Read the newest paid order and book a courier for it

steps.description: Shopify: read open, paid orders awaiting fulfillment.
Uber Direct: quote the run to the order's shipping address.
Uber Direct: book the delivery against the quote.

common.name: Market customer to newsletter (Square → Mailchimp)
common.description: Cross-provider: capture a customer at the stall on Square, then add them to your Mailchimp audience so the next market starts with an email, not a cold walk-by.

info.title: Market customer to newsletter
info.description: Square customer added to a Mailchimp audience. Cross-provider.

workflows.summary: Capture a customer, then subscribe them

steps.description: Square: create the customer record at the stall.
Mailchimp: add them to the audience (double opt-in via status=pending is the honest default).

common.name: Traceable batch to storefront listing (farmOS → Shopify)
common.description: Cross-provider: log a harvest in farmOS as the origin record, then list the finished batch as a Shopify product that carries the batch back to that log. Traceability from soil to storefront.

info.title: Traceable batch to storefront listing
info.description: farmOS harvest log linked to a Shopify product listing. Cross-provider.

workflows.summary: Log the harvest, then list the batch it became

steps.description: farmOS: record the harvest as the origin of the batch.
Shopify: list the finished batch, tagging it with the harvest log id so the listing is traceable to origin.
